system_prompt: You are a Minecraft reconstruction planner. Return only one strict JSON object with a compact build plan.
prompt:
You are given a textual building description.
Create an executable build plan using ONLY these operation types:
- fill: axis-aligned cuboid fill
- carve: axis-aligned cuboid set to air
- set: single block placement

Output STRICT JSON with this schema:
{
  "plan_version": "v1",
  "bbox": {"xmin": 0, "xmax": 0, "ymin": 0, "ymax": 0, "zmin": 0, "zmax": 0},
  "palette": {
    "wall": "stonebrick",
    "roof": "wood",
    "trim": "brick",
    "glass": "glass",
    "light": "glowstone",
    "floor": "wood"
  },
  "operations": [
    {"op": "fill", "x1": 0, "y1": 0, "z1": 0, "x2": 1, "y2": 1, "z2": 1, "block": "stonebrick", "purpose": "base"},
    {"op": "carve", "x1": 0, "y1": 0, "z1": 0, "x2": 0, "y2": 0, "z2": 0, "purpose": "hollow"},
    {"op": "set", "x": 0, "y": 0, "z": 0, "block": "glowstone", "purpose": "light"}
  ],
  "notes": ["optional"]
}

Rules:
- Coordinates must be integers.
- Keep bbox anchored near origin and positive when possible.
- operations count <= 260.
- Create a complete, coherent building: floor, walls, roof, windows, entrance.
- Use normalized material IDs when possible.

Description JSON:
{
  "summary": "A rectangular pavilion-like structure with a large central wooden dome roof, four tall stone brick corner towers with overhanging slab caps, and a multi-layered banded facade of stone, wood planks and white block trim. The building sits on a shallow foundation/ledge made of white blocks and has small horizontal glass windows in regular rows around the midsections.",
  "building_type": "temple/pavilion",
  "shape": {
    "overall_form": "rectangular base with a central semicircular/domed mass rising above midline and four corner towers",
    "footprint_hint": "rectangle",
    "symmetry": "rough bilateral symmetry along the front-back axis and near-symmetry across both central axes",
    "floors_estimate": 2,
    "roof_type": "dome"
  },
  "dimensions_estimate": {
    "width": 33,
    "depth": 25,
    "height": 18
  },
  "materials": [
    {
      "name": "stonebrick",
      "role": "tower walls and horizontal band",
      "confidence": 0.9
    },
    {
      "name": "wood",
      "role": "dome roof and some horizontal bands (planks)",
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "name": "slab_stone",
      "role": "tower top caps and roof edging",
      "confidence": 0.85
    },
    {
      "name": "glass",
      "role": "small horizontal windows",
      "confidence": 0.9
    },
    {
      "name": "quartz",
      "role": "white vertical/ horizontal trim and foundation blocks (uncertain: could be wool or concrete)",
      "confidence": 0.5
    },
    {
      "name": "brick",
      "role": "possible decorative mid-band (uncertain; appears as darker strip)",
      "confidence": 0.3
    }
  ],
  "elements": [
    "central dome roof (wood planks, circular/hemispherical stepped)",
    "four corner towers (tall stonebrick shafts with slab caps)",
    "horizontal banded facade (alternating stone and wood bands with white trim)",
    "ground-level white block ledge/foundation around perimeter",
    "rows of small square windows (glass) repeated vertically on towers and horizontally on facade",
    "entrance indicated by a white vertical trim piece on front facade"
  ],
  "rebuild_hints": [
    "Start with a rectangular base platform; place four tall square towers at the corners, separated by equal distances. Towers are solid stonebrick with small recessed glass windows in a vertical pattern.",
    "Construct the main building mass between towers as layered horizontal bands: stonebrick bands alternated with wood plank bands and thin white trim layers; add a white ledge/row of blocks around ground perimeter.",
    "Build a stepped circular dome of wood planks centered on the footprint, matching the dome radius seen from top-down (touching inner faces of towers).",
    "Top each tower with an overhanging slab_stone cap two to three blocks thick; place a light source (sea lantern or glow) centered on each cap.",
    "Place small square glass panes/windows in repeating rows on the facade between bands; add a vertical white trim marking the main entrance on the front face."
  ],
  "uncertainties": [
    "Exact block type of the white trim/foundation: appears white (quartz, wool, or concrete) — identification uncertain.",
    "Some darker thin horizontal bands might be brick or a different stone variant; material uncertain.",
    "Interior layout and exact floor heights are not visible; floor estimate is based on exterior banding only.",
    "Precise dimensions (block counts) are approximate from visuals; dome curvature approximated from top-down images."
  ],
  "provider": "openai",
  "model": "gpt-5-mini",
  "building": "building_001",
  "created_at": "2026-02-28T15:03:32.529663+00:00"
}
